item.title: I don't understand...
item.content:encoded: What is the anomaly in this entry? What makes it so that this is an SCP, and not a murderer? Is there anybody who can explain this to me?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For me, this fails the 'no, not really' test for determining if something should be a -J. The article is framed purely as a comedy skit rather than as a coherent document - all the addendums race to their punchlines without giving any clear sense of a real event being documented, and the dialogue in the interview only works within a comedy-sketch framing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oddly enough, I agree with everything stated above *but* the last log being too on-the-nose - I was still kinda of confused and expecting more.

…which is the point, but still - it feels oddly barebones.

I know it's a cliche, but I think this would've benefited from being a -J and letting it go full-on sillier.

No-voting for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, this warrants a rewrite; the core concept is one we've seen various spins on before but there's enough meat on the idea of splicing it with murder mysteries to make something worthwhile: this just isn't quite it yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Jam-con is over. If you're still interested, please rewrite this. I love the idea and I don't want to see it lost in the shuffle.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, if this wasn't my own skip I'd write something like "fourthing this" here. I'm planning on rewriting this piece because I think the base idea can work. But I can't touch it until the contest is over. Good to know that this article is probably worth retooling though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Same here. The last interview log was too on the nose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This started out a touch too abstruse for my taste, and ended up being a touch too silly. I want to like it, but it never quite broke out of novote territory for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I understand parts of you comments, I think you're misunderstanding one thing about the article:

SPOILERS
– hide block

There is a murderer. Theld confesses to the whole thing. In fact, she's SCP-3405.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I get that this is supposed to be a cognitohazard that makes the Foundation think these crimes are more complicated than they really are. But that isn't what you've given us. The fact that the supposed murderer doesn't actually exist (maybe? unclear) means that we don't have anything to focus on as an SCP.

Instead we're left with bumbling idiot detectives for no clear reason. That problem, combined with the self-aware tone of that last interview, makes the article read like a -j that falls flat.

Downvote for now, but I think this idea could be fantastic if you fixed those problems. It just needs to work as horror instead of failing to work as comedy.

item.title: And I Got Away With It Too!
item.content:encoded:
Jam-con entry that I pumped out. Thanks to Tanhony, MaliceAForethought, tretter, taylor_itkin, Varaxous, Stallmantic, and Kakroom for looking this over!

Um… something something meddling kids.

Obligatory author page link in case you want to read my other stuff.
